title: Rivian Normal Illinois Facility Production and Capacity
text: Rivian assembles its flagship R1 pickup truck, R1 SUV, and the commercial electric delivery van (EDV) at the 4.3-million-square-foot Normal, Illinois facility. Rivian acquired the plant, a former Mitsubishi Motors facility, in January 2017 for $16 million. The plant became Rivian's primary North American manufacturing facility. Rivian announced plans to make an electric delivery van (EDV) in three different sizes, capable of carrying 500, 700 or 900 cubic feet of packages. Two separate production lines exist at the Normal facility: one dedicated to the R1T/R1S models and one for the EDV500 and EDV700 commercial vans with both lines running in parallel. The company is expanding the factory by another 1.1 million square feet to accommodate production of its upcoming R2 electric vehicle. This expansion will eventually boost the facility's annual capacity to 215,000 vehicles. The plant presently employs 5,200 workers. The company expects to produce 25,000 vehicles at the facility this year. Rivian officially started R2 production at its Normal, Illinois plant just days after a tornado damaged the facility, with customer deliveries beginning this spring.
